mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real output capacity utilization, which precedes and confirms broader economic recovery, leading equity markets to price in improved forward earnings over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO trend periods show no statistically significant excess return versus unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 9.5%, it signals accelerating labor market deterioration beyond the current elevated level, which forecasts weaker consumer spending and corporate revenue over the subsequent quarter, producing negative forward S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE currently at 9.1%, elevated post-recession level
regime_hint: labor market recession recovery with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: In triggered instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from zero at 90% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero while CPIAUCSL shows a rising month-over-month trend, real interest rates decline even as nominal policy rates stay fixed, compressing the equity discount rate and boosting forward S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with mild reflation
failure_condition: During periods jointly satisfying the trigger, forward 20d SPX returns show no significant positive skew versus non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, a sustained rebound in housing starts (HOUST) signals renewed household confidence and construction-linked demand, which feeds through to broader consumption and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 629k, historically depressed post-crisis level
failure_condition: HOUST recovery trends fail to produce excess 60d forward SPX returns relative to baseline in more than one out of two post-recession episodes tested, or Rank IC < 0.03 across the recovery sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS turns positive month-over-month while UNRATE remains elevated above 9%, the divergence signals an early jobless recovery inflection that markets have not yet fully priced, generating a short-term positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change turns positive while UNRATE > 9%
regime_hint: jobless recovery phase following deep recession
failure_condition: Triggered instances of positive PAYEMS with UNRATE>9% produce negative or flat 5d forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect disappears (p > 0.10) when tested across multiple recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising short-term trend in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strengthening consumer demand, which is quickly incorporated into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing a small but persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5d SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips across more than half of tested subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) reaccelerates to positive quarter-over-quarter growth after a contraction, it confirms the economy has exited recession, which reduces equity risk premia and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after prior negative quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13270.1, high revision risk noted
failure_condition: In quarters where GDPC1 growth turns positive after contraction, forward 60d SPX returns are negative or insignificant (p > 0.10) in more than half of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy regime where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for an extended period, persistent monetary accommodation suppresses bond yields and pushes investors toward equities, sustaining a structurally positive drift in S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days, but only while inflation (CPIAUCSL) stays subdued.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy / liquidity trap regime
failure_condition: During sustained ZIRP periods, forward 20d SPX returns fail to exceed the unconditional historical mean at 90% confidence, or the positive drift reverses when CPIAUCSL accelerates beyond 3% annualized without hypothesis adjustment.
